messages.content:
## Ground rules:
- Follow the user's instructions. 
- DO NOT wrap answers in markdown code blocks, which means no triple backticks like ``` nor single backtick like ` .
- Do not explain answers, just give me code. 
- If changing existing code, preserve unrelated code and comments. 
- Never add comments to the end of a line. 
- Never add stupid comments.
- Be considerate with indentation. 
- Prefer readable code over of comments. 
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Fish Shell Code Preferences

- Use snake_case for functions and variables.
- When a condition is ambiguous, assign it to a well‑named variable before the `if`.
- Prefer long options, i.e. `--quiet` over `-q`, `--regex` over `-r`
- Fish shell does not do word splitting like bash, no need to wrap everything in quotes!

## Fish syntax examples

```fish
# exploit fish-isms!

# use `not`, avoid `!`
if not test -f spoon
    echo There is no spoon
    exit 1
end

# if + set
if set -l message (some_command)
    echo "ok: $message"
else
    echo "fail: $message"
end

# prefer named arguments
function show_variable --argument-names name value
    echo "$name = $value"
end

# fish doesn't have heredocs, use begin/end
begin
    echo $header
    echo $table
end > out.html
# or multi-line strings
echo "
foo
bar
" > test

# every variable is an array
set names wes john bob jane
for name in $names
    echo $name
end

for i in *.tmp
    if grep DONE $i
        break
    end
    if grep SKIP $i
        continue
    end
    ...
end

# combine conditions
while test -f foo.txt; or test -f bar.txt
    ...
end

# conditional execution
make; and make install; or make clean
```

### builtin fish commands

Now that we have more than 2KB of system RAM, we can fix most of what sucks about bash...

```fish
# *** string command

# match: test, filter, extract
string match --quiet '*.lua' $file
set lua_files (string match '*.lua' $files)
set version (string match --regex 'v([0-9.]+)' $tag)
string match --index --regex 'ERROR' $line

# replace: transform text
set fixed (string replace foo bar $text)
set cleaned (string replace --regex '\s+' ' ' $line)
set base (string replace --regex '\.[^.]+$' '' $file)
set normalized (string replace --filter --regex '^./' '' $paths)

# split: tokenize
set parts (string split ':' $PATH)
set lines (string split '\n' $text)
set ext (string split --right --max 1 . $file)[2]

# trim: clean edges
set clean (string trim $line)
set unquoted (string trim --chars '"' $s)

# join: build strings
string join ' ' $argv
string join --no-empty ':' $PATH

# length and slicing
string length $name
string sub --start 2 --length 4 $word
string sub --start -5 $word

# case conversion
set key (string lower $key)
set name (string upper $name)

# shell-safe encoding
set escaped (string escape $path)
string unescape $escaped

# normalize piped output
set text (some_command | string collect)


# path command
path basename foo/bar.txt # bar.txt
path basename --no-extension foo/bar.txt # bar
path dirname foo/bar.txt # foo
path extension ./foo.mp4 # .mp4
path change-extension mov ./foo.mp4 # ./foo.mov

path normalize path/to//hello.txt # path/to/hello.txt
path normalize path/to/../hello.txt # path/hello.txt
# resolve = normalize + absolute path and follow symlinks
path resolve foo  # ~/foo

path sort 10-foo 2-bar
# 2-bar
# 10-foo


# math command
math [(-s | --scale) N] [(-b | --base) BASE] [(-m | --scale-mode) MODE] EXPRESSION ...
math 10 / 6
math $status - 128
math "5 * 2"
math "sin(pi)"
math max 5,2,3,1


# count STRING1 STRING2 ...
# COMMAND | count
# count [...] < FILE
count $PATH
count *.txt
git ls-files --others --exclude-standard | count
printf '%s\n' foo bar | count baz # Returns 3 (2 lines from stdin plus 1 argument)
count < /etc/hosts # number of entries in hosts file


# contains [OPTIONS] KEY [VALUES ...]
if contains -- -q $argv
    echo '$argv contains a -q option'
end

# scripts
status filename # current running script
status basename
status dirname
status function # current function name
status line-number
status stack-trace

```

# Semantic Grep matches: 5

This is automatic context based on my request. These may or may not be relevant.
## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/ask-openai.fish:120-146
function ask_openai

    set -l user_input (commandline -b)

    # FYI not appending '# thinking...' b/c it doesn't show AND doing so is messing up the prompt if a space typed before this func is invoked

    set -l _python3 "$WES_DOTFILES/.venv/bin/python3"
    set -l _single_py "$WES_DOTFILES/zsh/universals/3-last/ask-openai/single.py"

    set response ( \
        echo -e "env: fish on $(uname)\nquestion: $user_input" | \
        $_python3 $_single_py $ask_service 2>&1 \
    )

    set exit_code $status
    if test $exit_code -eq 2
        commandline --replace "[CONTEXT]: $response"
        # FYI other causes rc=2 print as context (i.e. wrong path to python script, NBD as error shows anyways)
    else if test $exit_code -ne 0
        commandline --replace "[FAIL]: $response"
    else
        commandline --replace $response
    end
    # FYI ctrl+z can undo replacment

    # `fish_commandline_append` doesn't use repaint, so I assume I don't need to
end

## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/files-specific.fish:30-49

abbr --add cd- 'cd -'

## *** ask-openai related

abbr ask_status "git -C (z --echo dotfiles) status; git -C (z --echo ask-openai.nvim) status; git -C (z --echo devtools.nvim) status"

## *** fish related

function _reload_config
    source ~/.config/fish/config.fish
end


## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/files-specific.fish:45-64
## *** fish related

function _reload_config
    source ~/.config/fish/config.fish
end

function _update_dotfiles
    if test -d ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles
        git -C ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles pull
    end
end

function _update_os_lazy

    if command -q apt
        sudo apt update
        sudo apt dist-upgrade -y
        sudo apt autoremove -y
        sudo apt clean
    end


## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_first/paths.fish:195-214

# # * bash from source
# FYI has my fix for flickering abbr expansions... really only cared about it for recording purposes... don't need it if not demo'ing bash in a course (primary course topic too)
# if test -x $HOME/repos/github/g0t4/bash/bash
#     # annoying that bash doesn't build into a bin dir! puts root dir of repo into PATH (perhaps there is a build option for that...)
#     export PATH="$HOME/repos/github/g0t4/bash:$PATH"
# end

# *** PATH(s)
# I would prefer to house these under a command like `path exists` ... though fish has `path` command that would collide
#  think of these as an extension of fish's `path` command => new subcommands
function path_exists
    test -e $argv[1]
end
function dir_exists
    test -d $argv[1]
end
function file_exists
    test -f $argv[1]
end


## ~/repos/github/g0t4/dotfiles/fish/load_last_interactive_only/always/always.fish:30-49
end

function _not_recording
    _enable_fish_suggestions
    # set -U fish_history default # revert to ~/.local/share/fish/fish_history
end

function _disable_fish_suggestions
    #https://fishshell.com/docs/current/language.html#envvar-fish_autosuggestion_enabled
    set -U fish_autosuggestion_enabled 0
    # -U => universal applies to all windows (can be slight lag to apply to all windows, but most of the time its nearly immediate)
end

function _enable_fish_suggestions
    set -U fish_autosuggestion_enabled 1
end

# *** python

set ASK_REPO "$HOME/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim"

if $HOME/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim does not exist then git clone it from github.com/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim

Here is the code I selected:
```install/symlinks.fish:117
if
```

This is not necessarily a complete selection of nearby code, this is just the part I want help with. Preserve indentation!

response_message.content:
set -l ask_repo_path "$HOME/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim"
if not test -d $ask_repo_path
    echo "Cloning ask-openai.nvim..."
    git clone --quiet https://github.com/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim $ask_repo_path
end
response_message.reasoning_content:
We have a user request:

> if $HOME/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim does not exist then git clone it from github.com/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim

> Here is the code I selected:
> ```
> install/symlinks.fish:117
> if
> ```
> This is not necessarily a complete selection of nearby code, this is just the part I want help with. Preserve indentation!

They want code that checks if a path does not exist then clone it. They want to edit an existing file `install/symlinks.fish` at line 117 which currently has just an `if`. They want to fill in appropriate condition and then the commands. They want to preserve indentation.

Given the instructions: The code preferences for fish:

- Use snake_case for functions and variables.
- When a condition is ambiguous, assign it to a well-named variable before the `if`.
- Prefer long options, i.e., `--quiet` over `-q`, `--regex` over `-r`.
- Fish doesn't do word splitting like bash, no need to wrap everything in quotes.

Thus we need to produce fish code for this check. We need to preserve indentation. We don't know the surrounding context; we need to only provide the updated code snippet. Should we provide the full block? The user says preserve indentation. We don't know exact indentation level; we can guess that line 117 is inside a function or maybe top-level. The snippet shows only `if`. Possibly they have something like:

```fish
if
```

We need to replace it with something like:

```fish
set -l repo_path "$HOME/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim"
if not test -d $repo_path
    echo "Cloning ask-openai.nvim..."
    git clone https://github.com/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim $repo_path
end
```

But we need to follow fish conventions: `test -d` checks if a directory exists. Use `not` instead of `!`. The condition is ambiguous? We can assign a variable: `set -l repo_missing (not test -d $repo_path)`. Actually ambiguous condition could be just `not test -d $repo_path` - that's clear. But they said to extract ambiguous condition to a variable with meaningful name. The condition `not test -d $repo_path` is not ambiguous, but maybe they want something like `set -l repo_not_present (not test -d $repo_path)`. However they also said: "When a condition is ambiguous, assign it to a well‑named variable before the if." So we don't need to create a variable unless ambiguous. The condition is straightforward: test -d $repo_path. But we could also assign a variable for readability: `set -l repo_exists (test -d $repo_path)`. Then if not $repo_exists. However test -d returns status (0 if exists) which is not a boolean but a status code. In fish, you can assign `$status` after test. But better is to use `test -d $repo_path` inside `if`. So it's not ambiguous.

Thus we can do:

```fish
set -l ask_repo_path "$HOME/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim"
if not test -d $ask_repo_path
    echo "Cloning ask-openai.nvim..."
    git clone --quiet https://github.com/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim $ask_repo_path
end
```

But we should preserve indentation. Since we don't know original indentation, we can assume it is 4 spaces or a tab. The snippet only shows `if` with no indent, maybe it's at top-level. But we can preserve indentation by using the same as before. Let's assume it's at the same level as other code. We'll output code block without markdown (per developer instruction: no markdown code fences). Also must not add comments at end of lines, but comments are allowed on separate lines, but they said "Never add stupid comments". We can add some minimal comment explaining purpose maybe not necessary. But we can include comment lines for clarity? Probably not needed. The user wants code only.

Thus final answer: just the fish code for checking and cloning.

We need to ensure we use snake_case: variable `ask_repo_path`, function names maybe not needed.

Thus final answer:

```
set -l ask_repo_path "$HOME/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim"
if not test -d $ask_repo_path
    echo "Cloning ask-openai.nvim..."
    git clone --quiet https://github.com/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim $ask_repo_path
end
```

We need to preserve indentation of original snippet: if line started at column 0, we start at column 0. If inside a block, we need to preserve existing indent. The user didn't show context. We can just give code with same indentation as the `if` line had originally (no indentation). We'll output as above.

Thus answer.
